resources.string: These people will receive emergency notifications and your location.
You can disable location access for anyone at any time.
Are you sure you want to delete %1$s?
Please enter both name and email

"✨ Emergency Last Journal is not just an application, but a promise of safety.

❤️ Born from the hearts of those who wish no one to be alone in adversity, we created this tool with the hope that you 'will never have to use it'. But if the moment of destiny strikes, the application will be a silent gatekeeper, the voice when you cannot speak, and the thread connecting you with loved ones through every survival coordinate.

🌟 Every line of code is written with respect for the value of life. We believe that saving one person is saving a whole world. Thank you for trusting us to accompany you on every road."
You haven\'t added any emergency contacts. For the system to work effectively, please add at least one relative now.
No emergency contacts found to send SOS!
You haven\'t set up an email for your relative (%1$s). Please enter an email to send SOS notification:
Please set up basic information to be best supported in emergencies.
Where are you going? E.g.: Going home, trekking...
Tap to take a real photo
Update name, blood type and contacts
Do you want to end protection?
URGENT: 1 minute left! Sending SOS Email soon!
TIME OUT! SOS Email sent to relatives!
Take action immediately to help your relative.

string-array.name: survival_topics
string-array.item: Lost in the Woods (S.T.O.P)
House/High-rise Fire (R.A.C.E)
Lost/Disoriented
Flood/Rising Water
Tsunami/Tidal Surge
Earthquake/Structural Collapse
Storm/Tornado
Sunstroke/Heatstroke
Electric Shock
First Aid: CPR
First Aid: Acute Bleeding

string-array.name: survival_contents
string-array.item:
"Apply S.T.O.P rule:
1. S (Sit down): Sit down, stay calm.
2. T (Think): Think about the path you took.
3. O (Observe): Observe surroundings for signs of life.
4. P (Plan): Plan for shelter and create rescue signals (3 fires/whistle blasts).
* Note: Always find water and keep your body warm."

"R.A.C.E rule:
1. R (Rescue): Rescue people in immediate danger.
2. A (Alarm): Alarm, call emergency services.
3. C (Contain): Close doors to prevent smoke spread.
4. E (Extinguish): Extinguish the fire if safe.
* Skills: Crawl on the floor, use wet towels, don't use elevators. If trapped, go to the balcony and signal."

"1. Stop immediately.
2. Determine East (Sunrise) / West.
3. Find high landmarks (towers, mountains).
4. Follow streams/rivers as they usually lead to residential areas.
5. Mark your path with stones/cloth to avoid going in circles."

"1. Turn off the main power switch immediately.
2. Move property and family to higher ground.
3. Prepare food, clean water, and a first aid kit.
4. Avoid wading through water deep to the knees (water force can sweep away cars).
5. Monitor announcements from authorities via radio/speakers."

"1. As soon as you see sea water receding unusually fast, run to high ground.
2. Move at least 1-2km away from the coast or to a height of over 30m.
3. If trapped in a house, choose the highest floor.
4. Absolutely do not return to the coast after the first wave (later waves are usually larger)."

"1. Drop, Cover, Hold on.
2. Stay away from glass doors, large cabinets, bookshelves.
3. If outside: Stay away from buildings, power poles, large trees.
4. If trapped in debris: Don't scream excessively to save energy, tap on pipes/metal objects to signal."

"1. Find solid shelter, close doors tightly.
2. Stay away from windows, a sturdy wooden table is a good place.
3. If outside: Find a low ditch to lie face down, cover head with hands.
4. Be alert for flying objects."

"1. Move to shade/air-conditioned room.
2. Loosen clothing, apply cool compresses (armpits, groin, neck).
3. Drink water in small sips.
4. If unconscious, place in recovery position and call emergency immediately."

"1. Absolutely do not touch the victim directly before disconnecting power.
2. Use a dry non-conductive object (wood, plastic) to separate the victim from the power source.
3. Once safe, check breathing and heartbeat.
4. Call emergency services even if the victim seems conscious."

"1. Place victim on a hard flat surface.
2. Place hands on top of each other in the middle of the chest.
3. Press hard (5-6cm deep) and fast (100-120 times/minute).
4. Continue until medical personnel arrive or the victim revives."

"1. Apply direct pressure to the wound with clean cloth or gauze.
2. Elevate the injured area above heart level (if no fracture).
3. If blood soaks through the first layer, don't remove it, add a new layer on top.
4. Use a tourniquet only when bleeding is profuse and cannot be controlled at a limb."
